title: HubSpot's Revenue Hub Rebrand Is the Quiet Story of Q2: The CRM Now Runs Quote to Cash
desc: HubSpot renamed Commerce Hub to Revenue Hub on June 16 and shipped quote to cash: contracts, billing, MRR reporting, working capital, and an AR agent next.
keywords: hubspot revenue hub, commerce hub renamed revenue hub, hubspot quote to cash, hubspot contracts, hubspot capital, hubspot billing portal, hubspot revenue agent
cta: Want your sales and billing stack wired into one system?
body:
July 21, 2026. While the outbound world watched HubSpot's Prospecting Agent go wide, the bigger structural move landed at the other end of the funnel. Commerce Hub is now Revenue Hub, and HubSpot's own Q2 release roundup, published July 2, shows a quarter of shipping aimed at one goal: the CRM wants to run everything from the quote to the cash. Partner roundups this month, including Vantage Point's July summary, confirm the rename has already replaced Commerce Hub across HubSpot's product and help content.

What shipped, with dates

  1. The rename (June 16). Revenue Hub reflects the expanded scope: lead, deal, quote, order, contract, bill, collect, renew, and upsell, connected inside one platform instead of handed off between tools.
  2. Quote to cash went live (June). Contracts became generally available June 9 as a first-class object with TCV, ACV, MRR, and ARR built in. The Connected CPQ, Billing and Payments experience entered public beta June 16, with 902 portals enrolling in the first week: an accepted quote auto-creates a contract, the contract drives the billing schedule, and the buyer sets up payment at acceptance. A real-time MRR and ARR waterfall report shipped the same day.
  3. The money features. HubSpot Capital hit general availability June 16, offering Stripe-powered working capital to US and UK customers with funding in 1 to 2 business days. A self-serve Billing Portal entered public beta June 23. Paid invoices became editable June 18, automated quote reminders went GA June 16, and buy now pay later options via Klarna and Affirm arrived at checkout.
  4. An AR agent is next. HubSpot previewed Revenue Agent, an AI that will run invoice reminder workflows and account-based collections with a human in the loop, alongside the already-in-beta Breeze invoice prioritization that ranks overdue invoices by risk and value.

What it means for operators

Put the two ends together and the strategy is unmistakable. An AI agent now fills the top of the funnel, and an AI agent will soon chase the invoices at the bottom. The CRM is absorbing the revenue stack the way it absorbed the marketing stack a decade ago. For small teams, consolidation is genuinely attractive: fewer handoffs between quote, contract, invoice, and payment is where deals stall and cash leaks. The evaluation questions that matter: where does your quote actually live today, who owns your tax logic, what would migrating subscriptions from Stripe involve, and does your agency workflow need HubSpot's breadth or a platform purpose-built for agencies. That last question is real: GoHighLevel runs the same consolidation play for agency and local-business niches at a very different price point, and choosing between them is a positioning decision, not just a feature comparison. Either way, the era of the CRM as a passive database is over. If your pipeline still leaks between proposal and payment, that gap is now a solvable engineering problem, and fixing it starts with a lead flow designed to feed the machine.
faqs: The product was not removed, it was renamed. As of June 16, 2026, Commerce Hub is Revenue Hub, and all existing capabilities carried forward: quoting, invoicing, subscription billing, and payments. The rename reflects an expanded scope that now includes contracts, connected billing schedules, revenue reporting, and collections tooling.
What is HubSpot's quote to cash experience?

It is the connected flow HubSpot shipped into public beta on June 16, 2026: when a buyer accepts a quote, a contract is created automatically, the contract generates the billing schedule, and the buyer can set up payment at the moment of acceptance. Contracts carry TCV, ACV, MRR, and ARR, and a real-time MRR waterfall report reads directly from contracts and billing schedules.

Working capital financing, powered by Stripe, available inside HubSpot since June 16, 2026 for US and UK customers using HubSpot Payments or Stripe. Customers are pre-qualified from payment history, funding arrives in 1 to 2 business days if approved, and repayments deduct automatically from future transactions.
Should an agency move billing into its CRM?

Consolidation cuts the handoffs where deals stall and receivables age, and HubSpot's new stack makes a strong case for teams already on Sales Hub. But agencies should compare it against agency-focused platforms like GoHighLevel, which bundles CRM, funnels, and billing for client-service businesses at lower cost. The right answer depends on your client volume, subscription complexity, and how much of your stack you want one vendor to own.
